zh.Great Amwell, Hertfordshire, England: 英國賀特福爾格亞特 阿維爾
zh.lifeSummary: 當Sarah Warren於1613年4月19日之前出生在英國時，她的父親Richard Warren29歲，母親Elizabeth Walker29歲。她於1634年3月28日與John Cooke在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼普萊姆斯結婚。他們至少是5個女兒的父母。她於1623年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼。她去世於1696年7月15日之後在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼布里斯托達特姆斯。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sarah Warren于1613年4月19日之前出生在英国时，她的父亲Richard Warren29岁，母亲Elizabeth Walker29岁。她于1634年3月28日与John Cooke在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼普萊姆斯结婚。他们至少是5个女儿的父母。她于1623年移民到布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加普萊姆斯 可隆尼。她去世于1696年7月15日之后在布利提申 可隆尼亞爾 亞美利加馬薩瑟次 貝 可隆尼布里斯托達特姆斯。

nameMeanings.name: Warren
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin): from the Middle English (Old French) personal name Warin, Werin, a borrowing of ancient Germanic Warino, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. Compare Waring .

English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from La Varrenne in Seine-Maritime, France, named with a Gaulish element probably descriptive of alluvial land or sandy soil. This was the name of a major Norman family after the Conquest. In Ireland, this name has been Gaelicized as Bharain.
Irish: adopted as an English form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin (see Murnane , Warner ).

variants.name: Warrens
meanings: English: variant of Warren with genitival or excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Warrener
meanings: English (of Norman origin): occupational name from Old French warrennier ‘warrener’, an officer employed to watch over game in a park or game preserve. Compare Warren .

variants.name: Murnane
meanings: Irish (Munster): Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Murnáin, a shortened and dissimilated form of Ó Manannáin ‘descendant of Manannán’, name borne in Celtic mythology by a sea god associated with the Isle of Man. All three syllables appear in the variant Marrinan. This name has also been Anglicized as Warren and Warner .

variants.name: Warne
meanings: occasionally a habitational name from Wearne in Somerset. The place takes its name from a lost stream-name based on British verno- ‘alder, swamp’.

variants.name: Waring
meanings: English (Lancashire and Yorkshire): variant of Warren , from the Norman personal name Warin, derived from ancient Germanic Warino, a short form of various compound names based on the element warin ‘protection, shelter’ or ‘guard’. The name was popular in France and among the Normans (see Guerin ).

nameMeanings.name: Sarah
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: Biblical name, borne by the wife of Abraham and mother of Isaac. According to the Book of Genesis, she was originally called Sarai (possibly meaning ‘contentious’ in Hebrew), but had her name changed by God to the more auspicious Sarah ‘princess’ in token of a greater blessing (Genesis 17:15, ‘And God said unto Abraham, As for Sarai thy wife, thou shalt not call her name Sarai, but Sarah shall her name be’). This has been one of the most enduringly popular girls' names.

variants.name: Sara
meanings: Variant of   Sarah .  This is the form used in the Greek of the New Testament (Hebrews 11:11).

historicEvents.description: In 1620, after 2 rough months at sea, 102 Pilgrims landed at Plymouth Rock and established one of the very first English settlements in America.

historicEvents.title: The Pilgrims Board the Mayflower
historicEvents.description: "The Pilgrims boarded the Mayflower in search of religious freedom in the New World in September 1620. The Mayflower set sail from Plymouth, England, with 102 passengers, 40 of whom called themselves ""Saints."" After two stormy months at sea, the ship reached the New World. Nearly half of the group died during their first winter due to malnutrition and the harsh New England winter."

historicEvents.title: The English Civil War
historicEvents.description: A series of conflicts regarding England's governance during the years 1642 to 1651 is now known as The English Civil War. Charles I summoned supporters to join him against his enemies in Parliament. In October 1642, nearly 10,000 men fought for Charles I and chased Parliament across the River Tamar. Fighting continued for years and was finally ended at the Battle of Worcester on September 3, 1651, with a Parliamentarian victory.

historicEvents.title: Glorious Revolution
historicEvents.description: The Glorious Revolution brought the downfall of Catholic King James II and the reign of his Protestant daughter Mary and her Dutch husband, William III of Orange.

en.lifeSummary: When Sarah Warren was born before 19 April 1613, in England, her father, Richard Warren, was 29 and her mother, Elizabeth Walker, was 29. She married John Cooke on 28 March 1634, in Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. They were the parents of at least 5 daughters. She immigrated to Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America in 1623. She died after 15 July 1696, in Dartmouth, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sarah Warren est née avant 19 avril 1613, à Angleterre, son père, Richard Warren, avait 29 ans et sa mère, Elizabeth Walker, avait 29 ans. Elle a épousé John Cooke le 28 mars 1634, à Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique. Ils ont eu au moins 5 filles. Elle a immigré au Plymouth Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique en 1623. Elle est décédée après 15 juillet 1696, à Dartmouth, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, Amérique coloniale britannique.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Sarah Warren è nata il prima del 19 aprile 1613 nel England, sua padre, Richard Warren, aveva 29 anni e sua madre, Elizabeth Walker, aveva 29 anni. Lei sposato John Cooke il 28 marzo 1634, nel Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. Erano genitori di almeno 5 figlie. Immigrata nel Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America nel 1623. Morì dopo il 15 luglio 1696, nel Dartmouth, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ja.lifeSummary: Sarah Warrenが1613年4月19日以前にイギリスで生まれた時，父Richard Warrenは29歳，母Elizabeth Walkerは29歳であった。1634年3月28日にイギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニープリモウスでJohn Cookeと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも5人の娘の親であった。彼女は1623年にイギリス植民地アメリカプリモウス コロニーに移住した。彼女は1696年7月15日以後にイギリス植民地アメリカマッサチウセッツ バイ コロニーブリストルダルトモウスで死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Sarah Warren(이)가 1613년 4월 19일 이전 영국에서 태어났을 때, 그녀의 아버지 Richard Warren(은)는 29세였고, 그녀의 어머니 Elizabeth Walker(은)는 29세였다. 그녀는 1634년 3월 28일 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이 플림우스에서 John Cooke(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 다섯 명의 딸을 두었다. 그녀는 1623년에 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 플림우스 콜론이로 이민을 떠났다. 그녀는 1696년 7월 15일 이후 브리티시 콜론이앨 어머릭아 마작세츠 베이 콜론이 브리스톨 더르트모우스에서 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Sarah Warren nasceu antes 19 de abril de 1613, em Inglaterra, seu pai, Richard Warren, tinha 29 anos e sua mãe, Elizabeth Walker, tinha 29 anos. Ela casou-se com John Cooke em 28 de março de 1634, em Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. Eles tiveram pelo menos 5 filhas. Ela imigrou para o Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America em 1623. Ela faleceu após 15 de julho de 1696, em Dartmouth, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sarah Warren родилась перед 19 апрель 1613 г. в местности Англия, её отцу по имени Richard Warren было 29 лет, а матери, которую звали Elizabeth Walker, 29. Она вышла замуж за человека по имени John Cooke 28 марта 1634 г. в местности плимоут, плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика. У них было по крайней мере 5 дочерей. Она иммигрировала в плимоут колони, бритиш колоняль амирика в 1623 году. Она умерла после 15 июля 1696 г. в местности дартмоут, бристол, массачусетц беи колони, бритиш колоняль амирика.

Se casó con John Cooke el 28 de marzo de 1634, en Plymouth, Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America. Fueron padres de por lo menos 5 hijas. Inmigró a Plymouth Colony, British Colonial America en 1623. Murió después de 15 de julio de 1696, en Dartmouth, Bristol, Massachusetts Bay Colony, British Colonial America.
